channel.title: Vulncheck KEV
channel.description: Newly added vulnerabilities to the VulnCheck KEV database

item.title: [vulncheck] CVE-2024-20481 - Cisco ASA and FTD Denial-of-Service Vulnerability
item.description:
Cisco Adaptive Security Appliance (ASA) and Firepower Threat Defense (FTD) contain a missing release of resource after effective lifetime vulnerability that could allow an unauthenticated, remote attacker to cause a denial-of-service (DoS) of the RAVPN service.

Date Added to CISA KEV: 2024-10-24T00:00:00Z
Vendor: Cisco
Product: Adaptive Security Appliance (ASA) and Firepower Threat Defense (FTD)

VulnCheck Reported Exploitation Citations:
  • https://www.cisa.gov/sites/default/files/feeds/known_exploited_vulnerabilities.json
  • https://sec.cloudapps.cisco.com/security/center/content/CiscoSecurityAdvisory/cisco-sa-asaftd-bf-dos-vDZhLqrW

item.title: [vulncheck] CVE-2024-47575 - Fortinet FortiManager Missing Authentication Vulnerability
item.description:
Fortinet FortiManager contains a missing authentication vulnerability in the fgfmd daemon that allows a remote, unauthenticated attacker to execute arbitrary code or commands via specially crafted requests.

Date Added to CISA KEV: 2024-10-23T00:00:00Z
Vendor: Fortinet
Product: FortiManager

VulnCheck Reported Exploitation Citations:
  • https://www.cisa.gov/sites/default/files/feeds/known_exploited_vulnerabilities.json
  • https://cloud.google.com/blog/topics/threat-intelligence/fortimanager-zero-day-exploitation-cve-2024-47575
  • https://www.fortiguard.com/psirt/FG-IR-24-423

item.title: [vulncheck] CVE-2024-38816 - Spring Framework WebMvc.fn or WebFlux.fn Path Traversal Vulnerability
item.description:
Applications serving static resources through the functional web frameworks WebMvc.fn or WebFlux.fn are vulnerable to path traversal attacks. An attacker can craft malicious HTTP requests and obtain any file on the file system that is also accessible to the process in which the Spring application is running. Specifically, an application is vulnerable when both of the following are true: * the web application uses RouterFunctions to serve static resources * resource handling is explicitly configured with a FileSystemResource location However, malicious requests are blocked and rejected when any of the following is true: * the Spring Security HTTP Firewall https://docs.spring.io/spring-security/reference/servlet/exploits/firewall.html is in use * the application runs on Tomcat or Jetty

Vendor: VMware Tanzu
Product: Spring Framework

VulnCheck Reported Exploitation Citations:
  • https://cyble.com/blog/cyble-sensors-detect-attacks-on-java-framework-iot-devices/
VulnCheck XDB Entries:
  • https://vulncheck.com/xdb/79bbdef007d7 - https://github.com/masa42/CVE-2024-38816-PoC
  • https://vulncheck.com/xdb/802a28ccc80c - https://github.com/WULINPIN/CVE-2024-38816-PoC

item.title: [vulncheck] CVE-2020-11900 - treck tcp\/ip Double Free
item.description:
The Treck TCP/IP stack before 6.0.1.41 has an IPv4 tunneling Double Free.

Vendor: treck
Product: tcp\/ip

VulnCheck Reported Exploitation Citations:
  • https://cyble.com/blog/cyble-sensors-detect-attacks-on-java-framework-iot-devices/

item.title: [vulncheck] CVE-2023-20263 - Cisco hyperflex_hx_data_platform URL Redirection to Untrusted Site ('Open Redirect')
item.description:
A vulnerability in the web-based management interface of Cisco HyperFlex HX Data Platform could allow an unauthenticated, remote attacker to redirect a user to a malicious web page. This vulnerability is due to improper input validation of the parameters in an HTTP request. An attacker could exploit this vulnerability by persuading a user to click a crafted link. A successful exploit could allow the attacker to redirect a user to a malicious website.

Vendor: Cisco
Product: hyperflex_hx_data_platform

VulnCheck Reported Exploitation Citations:
  • https://blog.talosintelligence.com/akira-ransomware-continues-to-evolve/

item.title: [vulncheck] CVE-2024-38094 - Microsoft SharePoint Deserialization Vulnerability
item.description:
Microsoft SharePoint contains a deserialization vulnerability that allows for remote code execution.

Date Added to CISA KEV: 2024-10-22T00:00:00Z
Vendor: Microsoft
Product: SharePoint

VulnCheck Reported Exploitation Citations:
  • https://www.cisa.gov/sites/default/files/feeds/known_exploited_vulnerabilities.json
  • https://attackerkb.com/topics/Ev24ZWs2iT/cve-2024-38094#exploited-in-the-wild_9c3c0bdd-7a98-48de-a889-f351a2aec7cf

item.title: [vulncheck] CVE-2020-36831 - NextScripts: Social Networks Auto-Poster plugin for WordPress Multiple User privilege/security Functions Vulnerability
item.description:
The NextScripts: Social Networks Auto-Poster plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to authorization bypass due to missing capability checks on multiple user privilege/security functions provided in versions up to, and including 4.3.17. This makes it possible for low-privileged attackers, like subscribers, to perform restricted actions that would be otherwise locked to a administrative-level user.

Vendor: NextScripts
Product: NextScripts: Social Networks Auto-Poster plugin for WordPress

VulnCheck Reported Exploitation Citations:
  • https://www.wordfence.com/threat-intel/vulnerabilities/wordpress-plugins/social-networks-auto-poster-facebook-twitter-g/nextscripts-social-networks-auto-poster-4317-missing-authorization

item.title: [vulncheck] CVE-2016-15042 - Frontend File Manager and N-Media Post Front-end Form plugins for WordPress nm_filemanager_upload_file and nm_postfront_upload_file AJAX Vulnerability
item.description:
The Frontend File Manager (versions < 4.0), N-Media Post Front-end Form (versions < 1.1) plugins for WordPress are vulnerable to arbitrary file uploads due to missing file type validation via the `nm_filemanager_upload_file` and `nm_postfront_upload_file` AJAX actions. This makes it possible for unauthenticated attackers to upload arbitrary files on the affected sites server which may make remote code execution possible.

Vendor: N-Media
Product: Frontend File Manager and N-Media Post Front-end Form plugins for WordPress

VulnCheck Reported Exploitation Citations:
  • https://www.wordfence.com/threat-intel/vulnerabilities/detail/frontend-file-manager-40-n-media-post-front-end-form-11-arbitrary-file-upload

item.title: [vulncheck] CVE-2020-36842 - Migration, Backup, Staging -WPvivid plugin for WordPress wpvivid_upload_import_files and wpvivid_upload_files Vulnerability
item.description:
The Migration, Backup, Staging -WPvivid plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to arbitrary file uploads due to a missing capability check on the wpvivid_upload_import_files and wpvivid_upload_files AJAX actions that allows low-level authenticated attackers to upload zip files that can be subsequently extracted. This affects versions up to, and including 0.9.35.

Vendor: WPvivid
Product: Migration, Backup, Staging -WPvivid plugin for WordPress

VulnCheck Reported Exploitation Citations:
  • https://www.wordfence.com/threat-intel/vulnerabilities/wordpress-plugins/wpvivid-backuprestore/migration-backup-staging-wpvivid-0935-authenticated-subscriber-arbitrary-file-upload

item.title: [vulncheck] CVE-2017-20194 - Formidable Form Builder plugin for WordPress frm_forms_preview AJAX Action Vulnerability
item.description:
The Formidable Form Builder plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Sensitive Data Exposure in versions up to, and including, 2.05.03 via the frm_forms_preview AJAX action. This makes it possible for unauthenticated attackers to export all of the form entries for a given form.

Vendor: Strategy11 Form Builder
Product: Formidable Form Builder plugin for WordPress

VulnCheck Reported Exploitation Citations:
  • https://www.wordfence.com/threat-intel/vulnerabilities/wordpress-plugins/formidable/formidable-form-builder-20503-unauthenticated-information-disclosure
